llm_application_summary: KIVA-DEFLECT: deflector layer routes new incoming tokens to attention sink position to protect central focus subspace.
scoring_rubric: hit = kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7

results.title: Kivas — Colorado Encyclopedia
results.rationale: Source-domain reference describing the kiva intake shaft and deflector stone; not an LLM technique, excluded from prior-art assessment.

results.title: StreamingLLM with Attention Sinks (2023/9)
results.rationale: Foundational attention-sink work — preserving initial tokens as sinks is the substrate the deflector idea sits on; strong functional overlap with the protect-central-focus-via-sink motif, though without an explicit deflector-routing layer.

results.title: SinkTrack Context Anchoring (2026/4)
results.rationale: Injects/anchors context into the first-token sink — overlaps with using the sink position as a protective routing target for incoming activations, though direction is anchor-into-sink rather than deflect-into-sink.

results.title: Attention Sink Phenomenon (2023/8)
results.rationale: Original observation of attention sinks in autoregressive LLMs; provides the mechanism KIVA-DEFLECT exploits but does not itself route new tokens to the sink, so functional overlap stays below 0.7.

results.title: Anchor-Sink + Position-Decay Streaming Cache (2025/10)
results.rationale: Combines anchor sink with position-decay for streaming — implements much of the deflect-to-sink-to-protect-focus behavior via decayed routing; clear functional precedent.

results.title: Token Deflection to Sink for Streaming Inference (2026/3)
results.rationale: Direct match: an explicit deflector layer that routes disrupting tokens to the attention sink during streaming inference — essentially the same mechanism as KIVA-DEFLECT.

results.title: Central-Focus Protection via Sink Routing (2025/11)
results.rationale: Title and snippet name the exact framing: protect central focus subspace by routing new tokens to sink. Same goal, same mechanism, same vocabulary — near-direct prior art.

results.title: Outlier-Routing Stable Attention (2025/9)
results.rationale: Routes outlier/disrupting tokens away from main attention to a dedicated sink position — the deflection-to-sink mechanism under a different name (outlier routing).

verdict_rationale: Six of seven LLM-side results are hits, including two near-direct matches (rank 6 'Token Deflection to Sink' at func 0.93 and rank 7 'Central-Focus Protection via Sink Routing' at func 0.9) that describe the same deflector-routes-new-tokens-to-sink-to-protect-central-focus mechanism. Rank 8 covers the same idea under outlier-routing framing, and rank 5 already pairs anchor-sink with position-decayed routing. The candidate's own novelty_claim concedes that routing-to-sink is implicit in attention-sink architecture. Prior art is dense; KIVA-DEFLECT is not novel.
calibration_check: All assigned func scores fall within the provided per-rank calibration bands.
